015 */
016package org.consensusj.jsonrpc;
017
018import java.io.IOException;
019import java.lang.reflect.Type;
020import java.util.Arrays;
021import java.util.Collection;
022import java.util.List;
023import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;
024
025/**
026 *  JSON-RPC client interface. Implementations map JSON <i>responses</i> to {@link JsonRpcResponse} and JSON <i>results</i>
027 *  to an <i>expected</i> Java type specified by either a {@link Class} or by type {@link T}. In the default implementation {@link DefaultRpcClient},
028 *  {@link T} is Jackson's {@link com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JavaType}.
029 *  <p>
030 *  This interface is independent of the JSON conversion library (e.g. Jackson) and is independent of the {@link JsonRpcTransport}.
031 *  For historical reasons the interface is mostly synchronous, but {@link AsyncSupport} makes it easier
032 *  to add use of {@link java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture} for special cases. In the future
033 *  this interface may change to natively asynchronous.
034 *  <p>
035 *  Both JSON-RPC 1.0 and JSON-RPC 2.0 are supported. Implementations should also be (via configuration, perhaps)
036 *  lenient enough to support Bitcoin Core and similar servers that don't follow the JSON-RPC specifications exactly.
037 * @see <a href="https://www.jsonrpc.org/specification_v1">JSON-RPC 1.0 Specification (2005)</a>
038 * @see <a href="https://www.jsonrpc.org/specification">JSON-RPC 2.0 Specification</a>
039 * @param <T> Type that can be used (in addition to {@link Class}) to declare expected result types for JSON-RPC method calls.
040 */
041public interface JsonRpcClient<T extends Type> extends JsonRpcTransport<T>, AutoCloseable {
042
043    /**
044     * Return the JSON-RPC version used by the implementation
045     *
046     * @return JSON-RPC version
047     */
048    JsonRpcMessage.Version getJsonRpcVersion();
049
050    /**
051     * Call an RPC method and return default object type.
052     * <p>
053     * Caller should cast returned object to the correct type.
054     * <p>
055     * Useful for:
056     * <ul>
057     * <li>Dynamically-dispatched JSON-RPC methods calls via Groovy subclasses</li>
058     * <li>Simple (not client-side validated) command line utilities</li>
059     * <li>Functional tests that need to send incorrect types to the server to test error handling</li>
060     * </ul>
061     *
062     * @param method JSON RPC method call to send
063     * @param params JSON RPC parameters as a `List`
064     * @return the 'response.result' field of the JSON RPC response cast to type R
065     * @throws IOException network error
066     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status error
067     */
068    default Object send(String method, List<Object> params) throws IOException, JsonRpcStatusException {
069        return send(method, defaultType(), params);
070    }
071
072    default <R> CompletableFuture<R>  sendAsync(String method, List<Object> params) {
073        return sendAsync(method, defaultType(), params);
074    }
075
076    /**
077     * Call an RPC method and return default object type.
078     * <p>
079     * Convenience version that takes {@code params} as array/varargs.
080     *
081     * @param method JSON RPC method call to send
082     * @param params JSON RPC parameters as array or varargs
083     * @return the 'response.result' field of the JSON RPC response cast to type R
084     * @throws IOException network error
085     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status error
086     */
087    default Object send(String method, Object... params) throws IOException, JsonRpcStatusException {
088        return send(method, Arrays.asList(params));
089    }
090
091    default <R> R send(String method, Class<R> resultType, Object... params) throws IOException, JsonRpcStatusException {
092        return send(method, resultType, Arrays.asList(params));
093    }
094
095    default <R> CompletableFuture<R> sendAsync(String method, Class<R> resultType, Object... params) {
096        return sendAsync(method, resultType, Arrays.asList(params));
097    }
098
099    /**
100     * JSON-RPC remote method call that returns 'response.result`
101     *
102     * @param <R> Type of result object
103     * @param method JSON RPC method call to send
104     * @param resultType desired result type as a Java class object
105     * @param params JSON RPC params
106     * @return the 'response.result' field of the JSON RPC response converted to type R
107     */
108    default <R> R send(String method, Class<R> resultType, List<Object> params) throws IOException, JsonRpcStatusException {
109        return syncGet(sendRequestForResultAsync(buildJsonRequest(method, params), typeForClass(resultType)));
110    }
111
112    default <R> CompletableFuture<R> sendAsync(String method, Class<R> resultType, List<Object> params) {
113        return sendRequestForResultAsync(buildJsonRequest(method, params), typeForClass(resultType));
114    }
115
116    default <R> CompletableFuture<R> sendAsync(String method, T resultType, List<Object> params) {
117        return sendRequestForResultAsync(buildJsonRequest(method, params), resultType);
118    }
119
120    /**
121     * JSON-RPC remote method call that returns {@code response.result}
122     *
123     * @param method JSON RPC method call to send
124     * @param resultType desired result type as a Jackson JavaType object
125     * @param params JSON RPC params
126     * @return the 'response.result' field of the JSON RPC response converted to type R
127     */
128    default Object send(String method, T resultType, List<Object> params) throws IOException, JsonRpcStatusException {
129        return syncGet(sendRequestForResultAsync(buildJsonRequest(method, params), resultType));
130    }
131
132    /**
133     * Varargs version
134     */
135    default Object send(String method, T resultType, Object... params) throws IOException, JsonRpcStatusException {
136        return syncGet(sendRequestForResultAsync(buildJsonRequest(method, params), resultType));
137    }
138
139    default <R> CompletableFuture<R> sendAsync(String method, T resultType, Object... params) {
140        return sendRequestForResultAsync(buildJsonRequest(method, params), resultType);
141    }
142
143    private <R> CompletableFuture<R> sendRequestForResultAsync(JsonRpcRequest request, T resultType) {
144        CompletableFuture<JsonRpcResponse<R>> responseFuture = sendRequestForResponseAsync(request, responseTypeFor(resultType));
145
146//        assert response != null;
147//        assert response.getJsonrpc() != null;
148//        assert response.getJsonrpc().equals("2.0");
149//        assert response.getId() != null;
150//        assert response.getId().equals(request.getId());
151
152        // TODO: Error case should probably complete with JsonRpcErrorException (not status exception with code 200)
153        return responseFuture.thenCompose(resp -> (resp.getError() == null || resp.getError().getCode() == 0)
154                ? CompletableFuture.completedFuture(resp.getResult())
155                : CompletableFuture.failedFuture(new JsonRpcStatusException(200, resp)) // If response code wasn't 200 we couldn't be here
156        );
157    }
158
159    /**
160     * Create a JsonRpcRequest from method and parameters
161     *
162     * @param method name of method to call
163     * @param params parameter Java objects
164     * @return A ready-to-send JsonRpcRequest
165     */
166    default JsonRpcRequest buildJsonRequest(String method, List<Object> params) {
167        return new JsonRpcRequest(getJsonRpcVersion(), method, params);
168    }
169
170    default JsonRpcRequest buildJsonRequest(String method, Object... params) {
171        return new JsonRpcRequest(getJsonRpcVersion(), method, Arrays.asList(params));
172    }
173
174    /**
175     * Default no-op implementation of {@link AutoCloseable#close()}. Subclasses can override if
176     * they have something they need to close properly.
177     */
178    @Override
179    default void close() {}
180
181    T defaultType();
182
183    T responseTypeFor(T resultType);
184    T responseTypeFor(Class<?> resultType);
185
186    T typeForClass(Class<?> clazz);
187
188    T collectionTypeForClasses(Class<? extends Collection> collectionClazz, Class<?> clazz);
189
190    T collectionTypeForClasses(Class<? extends Collection> collectionClazz, T itemType);
191}
